sections.paragraph:
διωγμός, οῦ, ὁ (s. two next entries; Aeschyl. et al.; Polyb. 3, 74, 2; Plut.; LXX, AscIs) a program or process designed to harass and oppress someone, persecution (in our lit. only for reasons of belief) δ. μέγας a severe persecution Ac 8:1. μετὰ διωγμῶν (D-οῦ; other v.l.-όν) not without persecutions Mk 10:30 (s. MGoguel, RHPR 8, 1928, 264–77). ἐπεγείρειν δ. ἐπί τινα stir up a persecution against someone Ac 13:50; δ. ὑποφέρειν suffer persecution 2 Ti 3:11. καταπαύειν τ. διωγμόν bring the persecution to an end MPol 1:1. W. θλῖψις Mt 13:21; Mk 4:17; 2 Th 1:4; στενοχωρία 2 Cor 12:10. W. both Ro 8:35. W. παθήματα 2 Ti 3:11. W. ἀκαταστασία 1 Cl 3:2. DELG s.v. διώκω. TW.

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

AscIs AscIs = Ascension of Isaiah—List 2

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

RHPR RHPR = Revue d’histoire et de philosophie religieuses—List 6

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 253
